levels.body: "Afore" is a regional variation of "before" (cf. Gerson 300-01). Here, it suggests that Fern is speaking Dorset dialect, but other dialects also used this form in the nineteenth century. The word also appears in *Poems of Rural Life, in the Dorset Dialect* (1844) – one of the most prominent examples of Dorset dialect in nineteenth-century literature. In this collection, William Barnes spells the word "avore" (cf. Barnes 285).
worksCited: Barnes, William. *Poems of Rural Life, in the Dorset Dialect, With a Dissertation and Glossary*. London: John Russell Smith, 1844. Print.
Gerson, Stanley. *Sound and Symbol in the Dialogue of the Works of Charles Dickens*. Stockolm et al. : Almqvist & Wiksell, 1967. Print.
